title: MSc Cognitive Sciences, Learning & Technology
content: The M.Sc. in Cognitive Science, Learning and Technology is an interdisciplinary postgraduate program. It is newly launched Program from Department of Psychology at the School of Liberal Education, Galgotias University, that blends the scientific study of the mind with innovations in educational design and digital technology. The program is designed to equip students with the theoretical knowledge and practical skills to understand how people think, learn, and interact with technology, with applications across education, industry, and research. TheKey Focus Areas are, Cognitive Science: Understanding mental processes such as memory, attention, language, problem-solving, and decision-making. Learning Theories: Exploring how learning occurs across age groups and contexts—formal, informal, and digital. Educational Technology: Designing and evaluating tools and platforms that enhance learning experiences using technology. Human-Technology Interaction: Investigating how humans perceive, use, and adapt to interfaces, digital systems, and AI-driven tools. leading to socially relevant innovation and solving crucial educational challenges. To reach our educational goals and outcomes, the MSc program integrates interdisciplinary perspectivesranging from computer sciences, psychology, anthropology, philosophy, learning sciences, educational sciences, and neurosciences, offering a comprehensive curriculum. We offer cutting-edge programs that provide students with a multitude of opportunities to learn first-hand in an interdisciplinary environment. With our expanding industry partnerships, we aim to enhance research, practical applications, and placement opportunities to give students the chance to apply their knowledge and gain exposure to companies in their respective fields. We are committed to providing a comprehensive and immersive educational experience to help students succeed in their careers. This academic-lab-industry integrationprepares students for careers in research, cognitive and brain sciences (including AI), management, engineering, design, robotics,human- machine interaction, user interface optimization, and instructional design. Our students can enter different career paths in industry, global, educational, social, and government organizations. We believe that our MSc in Cognitive Sciences, Learning, and Technology encompasses an innovative and unique curriculum that aims at equipping students with an interdisciplinary blend of theoretical knowledge and practical skills, preparing them for diverse real-world applications to solve today’s and tomorrow’s education challenges, to foster equity, justice and quality education. Graduation with minimum 50% marks Students with a CUET score will be given preference in the merit list 01 Interdisciplinary Knowledge: Acquire integrated knowledge from psychology, neuroscience, linguistics, philosophy, artificial intelligence, and education to understand cognitive and learning processes 02 Scientific Inquiry and Research Skills: Develop the ability to frame research questions, design experiments, and apply both qualitative and quantitative methods to investigate cognitive and learning phenomena. 03 Application of Learning Theories: Apply principles of cognitive and educational psychology to create, evaluate, and enhance learning environments and instructional designs. 04 Critical and Analytical Thinking: Analyze complex cognitive issues using logical reasoning, problem-solving strategies, and data interpretation skills. 05 Use of Technology in Cognition and Learning: Employ digital tools, computational models, and neurotechnological methods (e.g., EEG, fMRI, eye-tracking) to study and improve cognitive and learning processes. 06 Ethical Awareness and Responsibly Demonstrate ethical sensitivity in research and professional practices, particularly in working with human subjects and handling personal or sensitive data. 07 Communication and Collaboration: Communicate complex cognitive science concepts and research findings effectively through written, oral, and visual means, and collaborate in interdisciplinary teams. 08 Cultural and Individual Sensitivity: Recognize and respect individual differences and sociocultural contexts in cognition and learning, promoting inclusive and equitable educational practices. 09 Innovation and Problem Solving: Develop creative, evidence-based solutions to cognitive, educational, and behavioral challenges in various applied settings (e.g., education, technology, health). 10 Lifelong Learning and Professional Development: Engage in continuous personal and professional development to keep pace with emerging trends in cognitive science, learning sciences, and educational technologies. 01 Interdisciplinary Knowledge and Skills- Equip graduates with a comprehensive understanding of cognitive processes by integrating insights from psychology, neuroscience, artificial intelligence, linguistics, philosophy, and education. 02 Research and Application-Enable graduates to design and conduct research that explores learning and cognition, and apply findings to address real-world challenges in education, technology, health, and human behavior. 03 Professionalism and Lifelong Learning- Foster ethical responsibility, critical thinking, and a commitment to continuous learning, preparing graduates for leadership roles in academia, research, and allied industries. 01 Apply Cognitive Theories to Real-world Contexts Apply theories of perception, memory, language, and decision-making to improve learning environments, educational practices, and human-computer interaction. 02 Conduct Interdisciplinary Research Design interdisciplinary research studies that combine insights from cognitive psychology, neuroscience, and education to address practical learning challenges. 03 Develop Evidence-based Learning Solutions Design, implement, and evaluate learning interventions and educational technologies based on cognitive principles and learner needs.
